title: Brainstorm-to-tasks pipeline: judgment gate and seed lifecycle
summary: The grill audited why a run-tasks session paused on pure design decisions (T-0001/T-0002) that should have been settled earlier. Root cause: the brainstorm loop lets unresolved judgment leak through grill -> seed -> to-tasks, where to-tasks (sanctioned by line 40) mints decisions as HITL tasks that run-tasks then halts on. The fix establishes a judgment/hands/AFK trichotomy, a hard durable state-based gate that forces every open_thread to RESOLVE/DEFER/REJECT before a seed can graduate, a new deferred bucket and structured out_of_scope, a disposable seed lifecycle with ADRs as the durable record, a grill-with-docs reconciliation pass, and the removal of the to-tasks scope quiz and save-location gate. Branching is explicitly deferred.
decisions: Adopt a trichotomy for classifying work: needs human JUDGMENT (a decision that changes what gets built) resolves in the grill and never becomes a task; needs human HANDS (a keyboard action the AI cannot do AFK, e.g. enable a flag, add a credential, configure DNS, seed data) is the ONLY legitimate task-level HITL; needs neither is AFK.

Keep the HITL label at the task level but narrow its meaning to hands-only. Remove 'architectural decision / design review' from to-tasks SKILL.md line 40 so the skill no longer sanctions decision-tasks.

Install a hard, durable, state-based gate. Every open_thread must exit via exactly one of three doors: RESOLVE (into decisions), DEFER (into a new deferred[] field), or REJECT (into out_of_scope). Nothing stays undecided.
Seed status is DERIVED, never hand-set: status is 'ready' iff open_threads == [], otherwise 'draft'.
to-seed refuses to finish a draft seed and loops the user back through handoff -> grill until every judgment thread is closed and status flips to ready.
to-tasks HARD-REFUSES a draft seed. This durable state-based check is the airtight backstop and survives context compaction, which a transient prompt did not.

Add a new deferred[] field whose items are { text, rationale, context, source_seed }. No revisit field and no dependency field: source_seed is the loose dependency hook, and real dependency modeling is left to the future consumer. deferred is record-only for now.
Restructure out_of_scope items to { text, rationale } so they read as negative context that to-tasks can consume.

open_threads holds genuine unresolved JUDGMENT only. Decided-but-conditional implementation details (e.g. 'use Registry API if jsonschema >=4.18 else RefResolver') are NOT open threads; they move into a task's acceptance criteria.

A ready seed is a fork point with two consumers: to-tasks reads decisions + spec for now-work and reads out_of_scope as a negative guard so it never scopes or tracers a rejected thing; a future ticket-gen skill reads deferred for later-work.

Provenance lives entirely in the disposable scaffolding layer (seeds, PRDs, task files stamp each other). Code and ADRs are the permanent record. Seeds are therefore deleted once the ENTIRE dependency chain is consumed.

clean-scaffolding becomes lineage-aware: it deletes a chain ATOMICALLY and only when the whole subtree is consumed (never partial, which would dangle refs), and it REFUSES to delete a chain whose architecture decisions are not yet captured in an ADR.
grill-me stays write-free: it interviews and produces a seed only, no doc writes.

grill-with-docs remains the sole ADR promoter and keeps its sparse three-criteria filter (hard to reverse + surprising without context + a real trade-off), which intentionally captures architecture-only decisions and lets mechanical decisions live in the diff.
No seed-to-ADR extraction step. Extracting from a compressed seed after the fact is lossier and context-free.

grill-with-docs gains a SESSION-END reconciliation pass: while the conversation is still live and decisions are final, it diffs the final decision set against the inline-written ADRs, supersedes stale ones, and captures architectural decisions reached late.

to-tasks removes the scope/granularity quiz entirely. On a ready seed it slices, classifies HITL/AFK, infers follow-ups, and writes autonomously. Its only remaining interactive pause is the draft-seed refusal.
to-tasks removes the save-location confirmation gate and writes straight to the derived docs/tasks/ path, ending with a single final output line: 'docs created here: <path>'.
Keep the HITL enum value rather than renaming it to MANUAL or INTERVENTION; only its definition changes.
Leave branching untouched this round. The branching prompt and shared branching-strategy.md are deferred to a dedicated future effort.
next_action: Run /to-tasks on this seed to break the pipeline redesign into tasks, folding the seed-schema changes into the in-flight provenance-contract work already on feat/contract-update.
problem_statement: The brainstorm-to-tasks pipeline lets design decisions leak all the way into generated task files as HITL tasks, so run-tasks pauses to ask the user to make architectural decisions that should have been resolved upstream. grill-me and to-seed do not force decision resolution, to-tasks line 40 sanctions decision-tasks, and to-tasks also adds scope-quiz and save-location friction the user never uses after a month of daily use.
solution: Define a judgment/hands/AFK trichotomy. Force every open_thread to resolve, defer, or reject before a seed can graduate, enforced by a derived draft/ready status with a hard refusal in both to-seed and to-tasks. Add a deferred bucket for later-work and structured out_of_scope as negative context. Treat seeds as disposable scaffolding deleted after the whole chain is consumed, with ADRs (via grill-with-docs plus a new reconciliation pass) as the durable record and a lineage-aware clean-scaffolding. Strip the to-tasks scope quiz and save-location gate.
evidence: A run-tasks session halted on T-0001 ('Decide to-prd-html producer status') and T-0002 ('Decide kind/ref correlation enforcement strategy'), both pure design decisions typed HITL. They traced verbatim to open_threads[1] and open_threads[2] in the originating seed (docs/seeds/20260603-1610-contracts-provenance-source-of-truth.json), which passed through grill -> seed -> to-tasks unresolved. The user reports over a month of using to-tasks and never meaningfully changing scope at the granularity quiz, nor ever overriding the recommended save location.
implementation_decisions: contracts/seed-schema.json: add deferred[] ({text, rationale, context, source_seed}); restructure out_of_scope items to {text, rationale}; add a derived status enum (ready|draft); tighten the open_threads definition to judgment-only. Fold these into the in-flight provenance-contract work on feat/contract-update; do not fork a parallel changeset.

to-seed/SKILL.md: route each open_thread into one of decisions|deferred|out_of_scope; derive and write status; hard-loop a draft seed back through handoff -> grill instead of the current non-blocking menu offer; stop mis-filing decided-but-conditional implementation details as open_threads.

to-tasks/SKILL.md: redefine HITL to hands-only and remove 'architectural decision / design review' at line 40; hard-refuse a draft seed; delete the scope/granularity quiz (lines 90-111); delete the save-location gate (lines 121-130); read out_of_scope as a negative guard; emit a final 'docs created here: <path>' line.
run-tasks/SKILL.md: HITL is now hands-only by construction, so the existing pause logic stays but decision-HITL can no longer occur.

grill-with-docs/SKILL.md: add a session-end reconciliation pass that diffs the final decision set against inline-written ADRs, supersedes stale ones, and captures late-reached architectural decisions.
clean-scaffolding: implement lineage-aware atomic chain deletion (never partial) plus an ADR backstop that refuses to delete a chain whose architecture decisions are not yet in an ADR.
Sibling emitters to-e2e-tasks, debug, and improve-component: inherit the hands-only HITL definition, and the draft-seed refusal where they consume seeds.
testing_decisions: No unit tests for these skill/schema changes, per the standing claude-code-shared infra convention (skills/agents/scripts work skips unit tests).

Validate schema changes with validate-schema.sh, including the in-file examples self-validation, and dry-run the grill -> seed -> to-tasks -> run-tasks flow manually to confirm the gate and the removed prompts behave as intended.
out_of_scope: Branching strategy redesign (single vs per-task and the shared branching-strategy.md used by 7 skills). Deferred to its own dedicated grill, not this pipeline.
Building the future ticket-gen skill that consumes deferred[]. The data contract is instrumented now; the skill is built later.
Any seed-to-ADR extraction mechanism. Explicitly rejected in favor of live reconciliation in grill-with-docs.
Migrating existing on-disk task files to the new hands-only HITL semantics. Not needed; these are disposable infra artifacts.
risks_and_tradeoffs: The seed-schema changes overlap the in-flight provenance-contract work on the same branch. Coordinating them in one effort avoids a fork but couples two changesets that must land together.

The hard gate relocates friction upstream into grill/seed rather than removing it. If a user runs write-free grill-me on architectural work, no ADRs are written and only the clean-scaffolding ADR backstop prevents losing the rationale on cleanup.
Deleting seeds after chain consumption makes provenance refs transient by design. Correctness depends on clean-scaffolding deleting chains atomically; a partial delete dangles refs.

The branching prompt remains a second interactive pause in to-tasks, so to-tasks is not fully prompt-free after this work. This is accepted because branching is an execution choice, not upstream judgment.
further_notes: deferred items use source_seed as a loose dependency hook; structured dependency modeling is intentionally deferred to the future ticket-gen consumer (YAGNI).

The grill-with-docs sparse three-criteria ADR filter is intentionally aligned with 'architecture standpoint only'; mechanical decisions (field renames, file deletions) are intentionally not preserved beyond the diff.
This seed was produced by a write-free grill-me session and itself has zero open_threads, so under the new rules it would be status: ready and safe to graduate to to-tasks.
